compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, I can align on LLM prompt design during brainstorm with prior-art research and behavioral review
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a prompt-alignment step to the scrutiny pipeline that triggers when LLM-instruction signals are detected — runs prior-art search on GitHub and dispatches bot-psychologist to review draft prompt text.
**Why**: Epics involving LLM prompt logic are especially sensitive to user intent due to non-deterministic execution. Without proactive review, prompt design gaps are discovered mid-implementation when they are expensive to fix.
**Scope**:
- IN: LLM-instruction signal detection with canonical keyword list (skill file modifications, agent definitions, prompt templates, hook behavioral logic). Doc-epic exclusion (Approach references only doc files). Provider-agnostic prior-art search on GitHub for similar prompts in popular AI/LLM projects. Collaborative prompt text drafting with the user. Bot-psychologist dispatch via Agent tool to review draft prompt. Findings incorporated into epic spec before approval.
- OUT: Changes to bot-psychologist agent definition (uses existing agent as-is, same dispatch pattern as fix-bug). Feasibility gate changes (Story 1). Follow-on scrutiny (Story 2). Planning-intelligence log (Story 4).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, epics with LLM-instruction signals trigger a prompt-alignment step in the scrutiny pipeline that includes a provider-agnostic GitHub prior-art search and bot-psychologist review of draft prompt text
  ← Satisfies: "scrutiny pipeline triggers prompt-alignment step"
- When this story is complete, the prompt-alignment step is conditional on a canonical keyword list with exclusion for documentation-only epics
  ← Satisfies: "bot-psychologist dispatch is conditional, triggered by canonical LLM-instruction signal keyword list"
- When this story is complete, bot-psychologist findings are incorporated into the epic spec before the approval gate is presented
  ← Satisfies: "findings incorporated into epic spec before approval"
- When this story is complete, the matched_keyword value is exposed as a named state variable for the planning-intelligence log to consume
  ← Satisfies: "planning-intelligence log records LLM-instruction signal engagement"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Bot-psychologist dispatch via Agent tool — same pattern as fix-bug llm-behavioral path. Test with and without LLM-instruction signals to verify conditional trigger fires correctly and doc-epic exclusion works.
- [Maintainability] The LLM-instruction signal keyword scan and the existing feasibility-reviewer integration signal scan are separate detection passes on the same epic spec text — both can trigger independently and compose correctly for dual-signal epics.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
